name: Port Internal Developer Portal MCP
what_it_does: Official Port MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Port's Internal Developer Portal — querying the software catalog, managing blueprints and entities, triggering self-service actions, and accessing platform engineering data for developer productivity.
use_cases: Querying service catalog entities from platform engineering agents
Triggering self-service actions (deploy, scaffold, provision) from developer agents
Managing blueprint relationships for dependency mapping agents
Checking entity compliance and scorecards from governance agents
Automating catalog updates from infrastructure change agents
Discovering team ownership and resource metadata from routing agents
not_for: Teams using Backstage, OpsLevel, or Cortex as their IDP
Simple service inventory without workflow automation
Direct infrastructure management (Port is catalog + workflow layer)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a Port-powered internal developer portal — especially for self-service actions, catalog queries, and platform engineering automation.
avoid_when: You're using Backstage, OpsLevel, or Cortex as your developer portal.

auth.notes: Port client ID and secret for OAuth2 machine-to-machine. API tokens available. Limited scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited users, up to 3 blueprints, basic features
pricing.notes: Generous free tier. Per-user pricing for teams. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Blueprint identifier required for entity queries — discover available blueprints first
Self-service actions are org-specific — agents must discover available actions
Entity properties vary by blueprint — dynamic schema inspection needed
Actions have required/optional inputs that vary per action definition
Webhook listeners for action triggers require publicly accessible endpoint
Port's entity model (blueprints, relations, entities) requires understanding before use
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 M2M tokens. SOC 2, GDPR. EU data residency available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 M2M. SOC 2, GDPR. EU data residency.
